svg.title: Garage door controller wiring: PoE ESP32-S3-ETH to isolated RS-485 adapter to Hörmann SupraMatic E2 BUS
svg.desc: Schematic showing a PoE switch powering an ESP32-S3-ETH board over Ethernet, an isolated Waveshare TTL to RS485 (C) adapter on the TTL side, and an RS-485 connection via an RJ12 6P6C numbered terminal strip to the Hörmann BUS, with isolation and safety warnings.

g.text: Garage door controller wiring — PoE ESP32-S3-ETH → isolated RS-485 → Hörmann SupraMatic E2 BUS
Use IEEE 802.3af PoE only — not passive PoE.
Isolation: do NOT connect ESP32 GND to Hörmann BUS GND when using the isolated adapter.
Adapter PE is the isolated RS-485-side reference (BUS GND). The ESP32 TTL-side GND stays separate.
Do not connect BUS +24 V to ESP32.
Do not connect ESP32 GPIO directly
Do not short A+ and B−.
Verify pin numbering with a continuity
Leave 120 Ω termination open for first
